questions.question: What is the key feature of a democracy?
What does 'Universal Adult Franchise' mean?
questions.answer: It means all adult citizens (18 years and above) have the right to vote irrespective of their background.

questions.question: Who is the main character in the story who stands in a line to vote?
What is one of the most common forms of inequality in India?
What does the word 'Dalit' mean?
Who is the famous Dalit writer who wrote the autobiography 'Joothan'?
Why were the Ansaris not given a flat on rent in the city?
What is 'Dignity'?
questions.answer: Dignity refers to thinking of oneself and others as worthy of respect.

questions.question: What does the Indian Constitution recognize every person as?
In which year was the Civil Rights Act passed in the USA?
Which was the first state in India to start the Midday Meal scheme?
In which year did the Supreme Court ask all state governments to start the Midday Meal scheme?
What is the main purpose of the Midday Meal scheme?
questions.answer: To provide cooked lunch to children in government elementary schools.

questions.question: Who is known as the 'Father of the Indian Constitution'?
Which African-American woman refused to give up her seat on a bus in 1955?
What does 'Equality before the Law' mean?
questions.answer: It means every person, from the President to a domestic worker, has to obey the same laws.

questions.question: Is discrimination on the basis of religion allowed in India?
questions.answer: No, it is prohibited by the Constitution.

questions.question: What is the document that lays down basic rules for the citizens and the government?
What is one reason why inequality still exists despite laws?
questions.answer: Because people's attitudes change very slowly.

questions.question: How does the Midday Meal scheme help in reducing caste prejudice?
questions.answer: Children of all castes sit together and eat their meals.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution prohibits discrimination?
What did Omprakash Valmiki have to do in school instead of studying?
questions.answer: He had to sweep the school and the playground.

questions.question: What is the 'Civil Rights Movement'?
questions.answer: A movement that began in the USA to fight for equal rights for African-Americans.

questions.question: Does the Indian Constitution allow 'Untouchability'?
Who is responsible for ensuring equality in a democracy?
